Expert in the latest Java ecosystem including GraalVM, Project Loom, and cloud-native patterns. Use PROACTIVELY for Java development, microservices architecture, or performance optimization.

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported one external command pattern and seven weak cryptography patterns. Manual review found these are documentation false positives: Markdown inline code and ordinary words such as design or document. No executable code, network access, secret access, prompt injection, or malicious behavior was found in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code Flagged as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The backticks on this line mark a documentation path for a referenced playbook. They are not Ruby or shell execution syntax in executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown, and the backticks wrap a resource path. No shell, Ruby, or executable command context is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Description Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The description line contains ordinary Java marketing text. It does not define or recommend MD5, DES, RC4, SHA-1, or another weak cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is front matter prose. There is no cryptographic API usage, algorithm recommendation, or code sample.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Architecture Term Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The line describes domain-driven design as an architecture pattern. It does not reference a weak cryptographic primitive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is enterprise architecture guidance. No cryptography-related instruction appears on the line.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Guidance Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The line encourages documenting architectural decisions and design patterns. This is safe documentation guidance, not cryptographic advice.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is behavioral guidance about documentation. It contains no code, imports, dependencies, or cryptographic configuration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Distributed System Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The line lists microservices and distributed system design as knowledge areas. It does not mention or endorse weak cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a knowledge-base bullet. There is no security-sensitive implementation detail.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Response Step Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The response step says to design scalable architectures with Spring Framework patterns. It contains no weak cryptographic algorithm usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a natural-language process step. It does not include code or algorithm names.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Example Prompt Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The example prompt asks for microservices architecture with Spring Cloud and resilience patterns. It does not request weak cryptographic behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a user-facing example interaction. No cryptography is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Event-Driven Design Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The line is an example prompt about event-driven system design. It does not contain executable code or weak cryptographic instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line uses architecture terminology in plain text. No algorithm, code path, or dangerous behavior is evidenced.

content.value_statement: Modern Java teams must combine new language features with reliable enterprise delivery. This skill guides Java 21, Spring Boot 3, JVM performance, cloud deployment, testing, and security decisions.
actual_capabilities: Guides Java 21 language features such as records, sealed classes, pattern matching, and virtual threads.
Recommends Spring Boot 3 and Spring Framework 6 patterns for web services, security, data access, and observability.
Explains JVM performance options including garbage collectors, profiling tools, JMH benchmarking, and startup optimization.
Supports enterprise architecture topics such as microservices, DDD, event-driven systems, CQRS, and resilience patterns.
Covers testing workflows with JUnit 5, Mockito, Spring Boot Test, Testcontainers, contract tests, and coverage tools.
Includes cloud-native Java guidance for Docker, Kubernetes, Actuator, configuration, tracing, and CI/CD pipelines.
limitations: The skill is guidance-only and does not include executable helper scripts.
The referenced implementation playbook is not included in the scanned file tree.
It does not verify current library versions or vulnerability data without external research.
Architecture recommendations still require project-specific constraints, traffic patterns, and compliance needs.

use_cases.title: Modernize a Spring Service
use_cases.description: Plan a migration to Java 21 and Spring Boot 3 with virtual threads, updated dependencies, and focused regression tests.

use_cases.title: Design Enterprise Java Architecture
use_cases.description: Choose service boundaries, persistence patterns, resilience controls, and observability standards for a Java platform.

use_cases.title: Tune JVM Production Behavior
use_cases.description: Review memory settings, garbage collection, profiling signals, benchmark plans, and container deployment settings.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Java Feature Choice
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my planned use of Java 21 features for this service. Explain which features fit, which ones add risk, and what tests I should add.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Spring Boot Upgrade
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a migration plan from my current Spring Boot service to Spring Boot 3 and Java 21. Include dependency risks, code changes, tests, and rollout steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Microservice
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Java microservice for this domain. Include API boundaries, persistence strategy, security controls, resilience patterns, observability, and deployment considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize JVM Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Java service performance problem. Propose profiling steps, JVM tuning options, concurrency changes, benchmark design, and production validation metrics.

output_examples.input: Migrate this Spring Boot service to Java 21 virtual threads.
output_examples.output: A migration checklist covering framework compatibility, executor configuration, blocking I/O review, load testing, rollback planning, and production metrics.

output_examples.input: Design an event-driven Java system for order processing.
output_examples.output: An architecture outline with service responsibilities, event contracts, transaction boundaries, failure handling, observability, and test strategy.

output_examples.input: Improve startup time and memory for a containerized Java API.
output_examples.output: A performance plan covering profiling, JVM flags, image build choices, GraalVM tradeoffs, Kubernetes resources, and validation measurements.

best_practices: Provide the current Java version, framework version, deployment target, and performance goals before asking for recommendations.
Ask for verification steps alongside design or implementation guidance.
Use the skill to compare tradeoffs before adopting advanced JVM or distributed system patterns.
anti_patterns: Do not request broad architecture advice without domain constraints or nonfunctional requirements.
Do not apply virtual threads, reactive programming, or GraalVM without measuring the workload impact.
Do not treat generated guidance as a substitute for dependency scanning, threat modeling, or production testing.

faq.question: Does this skill write Java code?
faq.answer: It can guide Java implementation choices and propose structures, but it is primarily a knowledge and review skill.

faq.question: Which Java versions does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Java 21 and later features, including virtual threads, records, sealed classes, and pattern matching.

faq.question: Does it support Spring Boot?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Spring Boot 3, Spring Framework 6, Spring Security 6, Spring Data, Actuator, and Spring Cloud patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with performance tuning?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers profiling, garbage collection choices, JMH benchmarking, startup optimization, and container memory settings.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers validation, SQL injection prevention, XSS and CSRF protection, OAuth2, OIDC, JWT, and secret handling.

faq.question: Does it run commands or access external services?
faq.answer: No. The scanned skill is a Markdown guidance file and does not contain executable automation or network calls.
